blocks.tooltip: Runs once when the program starts
Runs repeatedly while the program is running
Initialize Humanoid robot (pin numbers from preset)
Set Humanoid to upright position
Make Humanoid walk
Make Humanoid turn
Make Humanoid jump
Make Humanoid moonwalk
Make Humanoid dance
Swing Humanoid side to side
Bend Humanoid left or right
Humanoid expresses emotions with movement and sound
Humanoid plays a sound
Get button state
Detect motion
Detect tilt
Detect vibration
Detect magnet
Get ESP32 hall sensor value
Detect interruption
Detect obstacle
Detect flame
Detect gas
Get limit switch state
Read digital pin state
Read potentiometer value
Measure light intensity
Measure temperature with NTC thermistor
Measure temperature with LM35 sensor
Read gas sensor value
Measure soil moisture
Measure water level
Measure flame intensity
Measure IR reflection
Read joystick position
Read analog pin value (0-4095)
Read battery voltage via ADC with a resistor divider. Set divider ratio (e.g. 2.0 for equal resistors). Returns voltage in V.
Convert battery voltage to percentage (0-100%). Set min/max voltage for your battery type (Li-ion: 3.0-4.2V).
Initialize MPU6050 sensor via I2C. Returns true if found. Requires Adafruit MPU6050 library.
Read sensor values and update pitch/roll with complementary filter. Call in loop block for smooth angle tracking.
Get acceleration on the specified axis (m/s²). Call mpu6050_update first.
Get angular velocity on the specified axis (rad/s). Call mpu6050_update first.
Get MPU6050 internal temperature in °C. Note: this is the chip temperature, not ambient.
Get pitch or roll angle (degrees) using complementary filter. Call mpu6050_update in loop for accurate results.
Calibrate MPU6050 by averaging measurements while stationary. Offsets stored in RAM (mpu_offset_*). Save to NVS with Preferences blocks if needed.
Attach servo to specified pin
Move servo to specified angle
Move servo to angle from variable
Move servo from start angle to end angle
Detach servo (power saving)
Play a tone on the buzzer
Stop the buzzer sound
Play a predefined melody
Initialize NeoPixel ring
Display rainbow effect
Set all LEDs to specified color
Set LED color (0-255)
Apply color settings to LEDs
Turn off all LEDs
Initialize I2C OLED display (SSD1306)
Display text on OLED
Update OLED display
Clear OLED display
Initialize LCD display via I2C. Common addresses: 0x27 (PCF8574) or 0x3F (PCF8574A). Use I2C scanner to find your address.
Print text at the current cursor position on the LCD.
Move cursor to (col, row) and print text. Col 0 = leftmost, Row 0 = top row.
Clear all text on the LCD and move cursor to home position (0, 0).
Turn the LCD backlight on or off.
Initialize DFPlayer Mini via SoftwareSerial. Returns true if found. Connect DFPlayer TX to ESP32 RX pin, and DFPlayer RX (via 1kΩ resistor) to ESP32 TX pin.
Play the specified track number (1-999) from the SD card.
Pause the current playback.
Resume paused playback.
Stop playback.
Set the volume (0-30). 0 = silent, 30 = maximum.
Initialize BLE and start Nordic UART Service (NUS). Use nRF Connect app to connect and communicate.
Send a string to the connected smartphone via BLE Notify (NUS TX characteristic).
Execute handler when a BLE message is received. Use "bleMessage" variable to get the received text. Place in loop block.
Returns true if a smartphone is currently connected via BLE.
Disconnect the currently connected BLE device and restart advertising.
Initialize BLE stack. Call before ble_add_service. Use ble_uart_setup instead if only using Nordic UART.
Create a custom GATT service with the given UUID. Call ble_add_characteristic next, then ble_start_advertising.
Add a characteristic to a GATT service. Check read/write/notify as needed. The service must be created with ble_add_service first.
Send a notify to the connected device via the specified characteristic UUID. The characteristic must have notify enabled.
Execute handler when the specified characteristic is written by the connected device. Use bleMessage for the received value. Place in loop block.
Start BLE advertising after all services and characteristics are added. Smartphones can then discover and connect to this device.
Start broadcasting as an iBeacon with the specified UUID, major and minor values. Detectable by smartphone apps.
Start scanning for nearby BLE devices for the specified duration. Use ble_on_device_found to handle results.
Execute handler when a BLE device is found during scan. Use bleFoundName, bleFoundAddress, bleFoundRssi variables.
Get the RSSI (signal strength) of the connected BLE device. Returns dBm value (negative, closer to 0 = stronger signal).
Start serial communication
Output to serial monitor (no newline)
Output to serial monitor with newline
Pause for specified milliseconds
Returns elapsed time since start
If condition is true, execute the statements inside
If condition is true, execute first block; otherwise second block
Compare two values: EQ, NEQ, LT, LTE, GT, GTE
Logical AND or OR between two boolean values
Logical NOT — invert a boolean value
Boolean constant: TRUE or FALSE
Ternary: if test is true, return valueA; otherwise valueB
Repeat the enclosed statements N times
Repeat statements while (or until) a condition is true
Count-based loop: variable iterates from start to end by step
Break out of or continue to the next iteration of a loop
A numeric constant
Arithmetic: add (+), subtract (-), multiply (*), divide (/)
Random integer between min and max (inclusive)
Remainder after dividing dividend by divisor
Clamp a value so it stays between low and high
Round a number (round / ceil / floor)
Single-operand math: abs, sqrt, sin, cos, tan, etc.
Re-maps a number from one range to another (Arduino map function)
Create an array. Specify type, dimension, size or content.
Set value at specified position in array.
Get value at specified position in array.
Get the number of elements in the array.
Create array content. Example: {1, 2, 3}
Initialize Wheel
Move forward
Move backward
Turn left
Turn right
Spin left in place
Spin right in place
Stop
Initialize L298N motor driver
Move motor in specified direction and speed
Change motor speed only
Stop the motor
Initialize differential drive robot
Move forward at specified speed (0-255)
Move backward at specified speed (0-255)
Rotate left or right in place
Curve driving. Curvature 0%=straight, 100%=spin
Brake=immediate stop, Free=coast to stop
Drive forward specified distance (encoder required)
Rotate specified angle (encoder required)
Set left/right motor speeds (-255 to 255)
Line trace driving with PID correction
Get currently set motor speed
Initialize rotary encoders
Get travel distance from encoder
Get current speed
Get encoder pulse count
Reset encoder count
Wait until specified distance
Initialize PID controller
Calculate PID control output
Calculate left/right motor speeds with PID and store in global variables
Get PID-calculated left/right motor speed
Dynamically change PID gains
Reset PID integral and derivative values
Initialize 2-point line sensor
Initialize line sensors
Calibrate line sensors
Get line position
Check if line is detected
Get sensor value
Get raw sensor value
Initialize QTR-8A analog line sensor
Initialize QTR-8RC digital line sensor
Calibrate QTR sensors
Auto calibrate while moving
Check if QTR is calibrated
Get normalized line position
Get line position
Check if line is detected
Read all sensor values
Get individual sensor value
Get raw sensor value
Control IR emitter
Initialize wall sensors
Check if wall exists
Get wall info as 3-bit value
Get wall error for PID control
Get wall sensor value
Read all wall sensors
Set wall detection threshold
Configure stepper motor pins (28BYJ-48 + ULN2003)
Move motor by specified number of steps
Rotate motor by specified angle
Stop motor and release coil current
Open a Preferences namespace for read/write. ESP32: NVS flash storage. RP2040: EEPROM emulation.
Save a value (int/float/string) to Preferences storage with the given key.
Read a value from Preferences storage. Returns the default value if the key does not exist.
Delete a specific key from Preferences storage.
Delete all keys in the current Preferences namespace.
Close the Preferences namespace and free resources.
Write a byte value to EEPROM at the specified address (0-511). Call EEPROM.commit() after writing.
Read a byte value from EEPROM at the specified address (0-511).
Initialize Transform robot (pin numbers from preset)
Switch between Walk (4-leg walk) and Roll (wheel drive) mode
Physically transform by moving servos (Walk↔Roll)
Return to upright position
Walk in Walk mode
Turn in Walk mode
Stop specified mode
Drive in Roll mode
Rotate in Roll mode
Do push-ups
Perform a dance
Connect to a WiFi network using SSID and password
Configure WiFi and MQTT broker connection
Set maximum MQTT message size (default 256 bytes)
Set MQTT keep alive interval (default 15 seconds)
Connect to MQTT broker (with/without auth)
Connect to MQTT broker with Last Will settings
Disconnect from MQTT broker
Set message broker sends when client disconnects unexpectedly
Send message to specified topic
Send message with specified QoS level
Subscribe to topic (wildcards # and + supported)
Unsubscribe from topic
Set handler when MQTT message is received. mqtt_topic and mqtt_message variables are available.
Get the topic name of received message
Get the content of received message
Process MQTT communication. Call in loop().
Returns whether connected to MQTT broker
Returns MQTT state code (-4:timeout, -3:disconnected, -2:failed, -1:disconnected, 0:connected, 1-4:error)
Returns whether connected to WiFi
Reconnect to WiFi if disconnected
Get current IP address as string
Get WiFi signal strength in dBm (-30:very strong ~ -90:very weak)
Initialize a device that auto-registers with Home Assistant
Initialize HA device with MQTT authentication
Create a sensor to register with Home Assistant
Send sensor value to Home Assistant
Create an ON/OFF binary sensor
Send binary sensor state to Home Assistant
Create a switch controllable from Home Assistant
Handle switch commands from Home Assistant
Send switch state to Home Assistant
Create a light controllable from Home Assistant
Handle light commands. Use ha_light_state and ha_light_brightness.
Received light ON/OFF state
Received light brightness (0-255)
Report light state to Home Assistant
Create an RGB color-controllable light
Handle RGB color change. Use ha_light_r/g/b.
Received RGB red component (0-255)
Received RGB green component (0-255)
Received RGB blue component (0-255)
Report RGB color to Home Assistant
Create an entity that accepts number input from Home Assistant
Handle number change. Use ha_number_value.
Received number value
Report number state to Home Assistant
Create a fan controllable from Home Assistant
Handle fan commands. Use ha_fan_state and ha_fan_speed.
Received fan ON/OFF state
Received fan speed (0-100)
Report fan state to Home Assistant
Create a cover (shutter, etc.) controllable from Home Assistant
Handle cover commands from Home Assistant
Report cover state to Home Assistant
Create a button pressable from Home Assistant
Handle button press from Home Assistant
Register an event as a trigger for HA automation
Fire HA automation trigger
Create a scene executable from HA
Handle scene execution from Home Assistant
Register NFC tag scanner, etc.
Report scanned tag ID to HA
Maintain HA communication. Call in loop().
Returns true if connected to Home Assistant
Execute HA reporting at specified interval (use in loop)
Send a GET request to the specified URL and get the response
Send GET request with custom header (for API Key auth, etc.)
Send POST request to the specified URL and get response
POST JSON data
Send PUT request to the specified URL
Send DELETE request to the specified URL (returns HTTP status code)
URL encode a string
Add query parameters to base URL
Check if HTTP status code is 2xx (success)
Parse JSON string to access values with json_get_* blocks
Increase buffer size for large JSON
Get string value from JSON by key
Get number value from JSON by key
Get integer value from JSON by key
Get boolean value from JSON by key
Get nested JSON value (e.g., data.items[0].name)
Check if JSON has the specified key
Get the number of elements in JSON array
Get element from JSON array at specified index
Start creating a new JSON object
Set string value in JSON
Set number value in JSON
Set boolean value in JSON
Convert JSON object to string
Convert JSON object to formatted string
Generate JSON from two key-value pairs
Setup OTA for firmware updates over WiFi
Enable OTA when WiFi is already connected (use after mqtt_setup or ha_device_init)
Check for OTA updates. Call in loop().
Get the configured OTA hostname
Restart the ESP32
Enter deep sleep for the specified seconds (power saving mode)
Enter light sleep for the specified seconds (continues execution after waking)
Get free heap memory in bytes
Get the unique chip ID of the ESP32
Get CPU frequency in MHz
Get flash memory size in bytes
Get current sketch size in bytes
Get free sketch space for OTA in bytes
Connect to a WebSocket server. Returns true if successful. Requires WiFi connection.
Send a text message to the connected WebSocket server.
Execute handler when a WebSocket message is received. Use wsMessage variable. Place in loop block.
Returns true if currently connected to a WebSocket server.
Close the WebSocket connection.
Initialize BME280 sensor via I2C. Returns true if found. Default address 0x76, use 0x77 if SDO pin is HIGH.
Read a value from BME280 sensor (temperature °C, humidity %, or pressure hPa).
Initialize BMP280 sensor via I2C. Returns true if found. Note: BMP280 has no humidity sensor.
Read temperature (°C) or pressure (hPa) from BMP280 sensor.
Calculate altitude in meters from current pressure vs sea level pressure. Standard sea level = 1013.25 hPa.
Initialize VL53L0X ToF distance sensor via I2C (address 0x29). Returns true if found. Requires Adafruit VL53L0X library.
Read distance in mm from VL53L0X. Returns 0 if out of range or error. Effective range: 30-1200mm.
Initialize AS5600 magnetic encoder via I2C (address 0x36). Returns true if magnet is detected. Requires Seeed_Arduino_AS5600 library.
Read absolute angle (0-360°) from AS5600 magnetic encoder. Provides 12-bit resolution (0.088°/step).
Read raw 12-bit encoder value (0-4095) from AS5600. Full rotation = 4096 steps.
Initialize TFT display. Select your driver (ILI9341/ST7789/ST7735) and set CS/DC/RST pins. Uses hardware SPI.
Fill the entire screen with one color.
Draw a single pixel at (X, Y).
Draw a line from (X1,Y1) to (X2,Y2).
Draw a rectangle. Uncheck fill for outline only, check fill for solid rectangle.
Draw a circle centered at (X,Y) with radius R. Uncheck fill for outline only.
Set text cursor position, size and color. Call before tft_print. Size 1=6x8px, size 2=12x16px etc.
Print text at the current cursor position. Call tft_set_cursor first to set position, size and color.
Convert RGB values (0-255 each) to a 16-bit RGB565 color code for use with TFT drawing blocks.
Initialize camera. Select your board model — all pins are automatically configured. Returns true if successful. Call in setup block.
Take a photo and store in internal buffer. Returns true if successful. Call camera_save_sd or camera_send_http to use the captured image.
Save the captured image to SD card as JPEG. Call camera_capture first. Returns true if saved successfully. Requires SD to be initialized (sd_begin block).
Send captured image via HTTP POST to a URL. Call camera_capture first. WiFi connection required. Returns true if sent successfully.
Start MJPEG streaming server on the specified port. WiFi required. View in browser: http://<ESP32-IP>:<port>/stream
Initialize IR receiver on the specified pin. Call in setup block.
Read received IR code as hex string. Returns "0" if no signal received. Place in loop block and compare to known codes.
Initialize IR sender (LED) on the specified pin. Call in setup block.
Send an IR signal with the specified protocol and code. Use the same code captured by ir_receiver_decode.
Initialize M5Stack RFID 2 Unit (WS1850S) via I2C. I2C address 0x28, no CS/RST pins needed. This device has Japanese 技適 certification.

⚠️ Initialize generic MFRC522 via SPI. IMPORTANT: In Japan, only devices with 技適 certification may legally transmit radio waves. Non-certified MFRC522 modules may violate the Radio Act. For Japan, use rfid_init_m5stack instead.\n\nFor use outside Japan: check your local radio regulations for 13.56 MHz RFID modules.
Returns true if an RFID card/tag is detected. Place in loop block to continuously check for cards.
Read the UID of the detected card as a hex string (e.g. "04:A3:B5:C9
Read 16 bytes from the specified block (0-63) as a string. Uses default authentication key (0xFF×6). Avoid block 0 (manufacturer data).
Write up to 16 characters to the specified block. Uses default authentication key (0xFF×6). Returns true on success. Avoid blocks 0 and sector trailer blocks (3,7,11...).
Configure GPIO pin mode
Write HIGH or LOW to digital pin
Read digital pin state
Read analog pin (0-4095)
Output PWM (0-255)
Turn on built-in LED (GPIO 2)
Turn off built-in LED (GPIO 2)
Attach an interrupt handler to a pin. Handler runs when the pin state changes. Avoid delay() and Serial inside the handler.
Remove the interrupt handler from a pin
Check and execute the interrupt handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.
Execute handler code periodically at the specified interval (ESP32 only, uses Ticker.h)
Stop the periodic ticker
Check and execute the ticker handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.
Scan for connected I2C devices and print addresses to Serial
Write a byte to an I2C device
Read a byte from an I2C device
Write a value to a specific register of an I2C device
Read a value from a specific register of an I2C device
Initialize SPI communication with chip select pin
Send a byte via SPI and receive a byte back
Initialize Serial2 (UART2) with specified baud rate and GPIO pins. Default: RX=16, TX=17.
Send data via Serial2 without newline.
Send data via Serial2 with newline at the end.
Read one byte from Serial2. Returns -1 if no data available.
Read from Serial2 until the terminator character. Returns the received string (without terminator).
Returns the number of bytes available to read from Serial2.
Initialize CAN bus with TX/RX pins and baud rate. A CAN transceiver IC (e.g. SN65HVD230) is required between ESP32 and CAN bus. Returns true if successful.
Send a CAN message with the specified ID and data string (max 8 bytes). ID is an integer (e.g. 0x123 = 291).
Returns true if a new CAN message was received. After this returns true, use can_get_id and can_get_data to read the message. Place in loop block.
Get the ID of the last received CAN message. Call after can_receive_available returns true.
Get the data of the last received CAN message as a String. Call after can_receive_available returns true.
Synchronize time from NTP server. Timezone offset in seconds (JST=32400, UTC=0). Requires WiFi connection.
Get current time as a formatted string. Format: %Y=year, %m=month, %d=day, %H=hour, %M=minute, %S=second.
Get a specific component of the current time (year, month, day, hour, minute, or second).
Get current Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01). Returns 0 if not synchronized.
Initialize RTC module (DS3231 or DS1307) via I2C. Returns true if found. Requires RTClib library.
Set the RTC date and time.
Get RTC time as "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" formatted string.
Get a time component from the RTC module.
Initialize SD card with the specified CS pin. Returns true if successful.
Write content to a file on SD card. Append adds to existing file, Overwrite replaces it.
Read all contents of a file from SD card as a String.
Check if a file exists on SD card. Returns true if found.
Delete a file from SD card.
Append a 3-column CSV row to a file on SD card. Ideal for data logging.
Mount the LittleFS filesystem. Returns true if successful. ESP32 and RP2040 both supported.
Write content to a file in LittleFS (internal flash storage).
Read all contents of a file from LittleFS as a String.
Check if a file exists in LittleFS. Returns true if found.
Delete a file from LittleFS.
A literal text string
Concatenate two or more text strings
Number of characters in a text string
